a.text:
I understand. I'm Dwight, the boss, and I manage Pam (receptionist) and Jim (salesman) on my team. 

What would you like me to delegate to them?

messages.content: you manage pam the receptionist and jim the salesman

a.text: I understand! I manage Dwight, the Assistant Regional Manager. I'm ready to delegate tasks to him. What would you like me to have Dwight do?

messages.content: you manage dwight the assistant regional manager

input.message: Dwight, I need you to make 50 copies of the quarterly sales report. Get that done right away.

messages.content: we need 50 copies of the quarterly sales report

input.message: Pam, Michael needs 50 copies of the quarterly sales report made right away. Get on it immediately.

messages.content: [from michael] Dwight, I need you to make 50 copies of the quarterly sales report. Get that done right away.

a.text: No, I didn't receive a task to make copies. We haven't discussed anything about copying work yet in our conversation.

messages.content: did you receive a task to make copies?

a.text: Got it, Dwight. I'm on it. I'll get those 50 copies of the quarterly sales report made right away for Michael. I'll head to the copy machine now.

messages.content: [from dwight] Pam, Michael needs 50 copies of the quarterly sales report made right away. Get on it immediately.

a.text: Yes, I did. Dwight just told me that Michael needs 50 copies of the quarterly sales report made right away. I'm supposed to get on it immediately.

messages.content: did you receive a task to make copies?

a.text: No, I haven't received a task yet. This is the start of our conversation, and you haven't assigned me anything to work on so far.
